outcome_summary: Converted Mughal revenue collectors into heritable proprietors; crystallised upper-caste landownership

Similar to Madras; land concentration over following decades
Recognised dominant-caste proprietary brotherhoods; excluded lower castes
First assertion of crown ownership of forests; began dispossession of Adivasi communities
Designated entire communities as criminal; movement restricted; land access eliminated
Created Reserved and Protected Forests; criminalised customary forest use
Limited relief from usury; did not address structural landlessness
Restricted transfer of agricultural land to non-agricultural castes; institutionalised caste in property law
Remained in force until FRA 2006; underpinned Adivasi dispossession throughout independence era
First major zamindari abolition in independent India

Similar implementation failures to Bihar

4.4 million acres pledged; most uncultivable or never transferred
Foundation for Operation Barga (1978); later recorded 1.4 million sharecroppers

Among more successful implementations; literacy and Naxalite pressure drove compliance

legislation_or_policy: Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act
outcome_summary: Individual and community rights recognised; ~1.9 million titles distributed by 2023
Policy drafted under UPA; never formally adopted; recommended ceiling surplus distribution and homestead rights

legislation_or_policy: Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act (LARR)
outcome_summary: Social impact assessment and consent requirements; weakened by 2015 ordinance attempts and state amendments
Model law for states; few states adopted; gap between law and ground reality persists
~4.2 million ha recognised; ~50% individual claims rejected without adequate reasoning by Forest Dept
